text:
There once was a small village nestled between rolling hills. In the village there lived a humble young man named Everdingle. Everdingle spent almost his entire day tending to his small garden. Every morning he would carefully walk his chickens across the meadow, water their feathers, play with ducks, and gather wildflowers.

Every evening he would sit by the fire, listening to the soft sounds of the village life. He imagined fantastical tales that couldn't happen here. Once a week, he'd trade a couple of berries for news from the neighboring town—or just dream aloud.

One day, while picking mint leaves for tea, Everdingle stumbled upon a mysterious old book buried beneath an ancient oak tree. Its leather cover was faded, but in bold script on the front was written:

"A book of wonders lost to time."

Everdingle didn't dare to read it. Who knew how vivid these stories were? Instead, he decided to learn the village folklore. He'd heard tales
